title: Immunomagnetic Labeling and Separation of Escherichia coli Using Protein A-Coated Magnetic Nanoparticles
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f757r","text":"This protocol outlines a method for the immunomagnetic labeling and separation of Escherichia coli ATCC 25922 using Protein A-coated magnetic nanoparticles (MNPs). The procedure involves culturing E. coli, preparing a bacterial suspension, antibody labeling, and subsequent capture using MNPs. The efficiency of bacterial capture is assessed by plate counting, providing a quantitative measure of the labeling process. This method is suitable for applications requiring the isolation of labeled bacterial populations for downstream analyses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":82,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":197,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qk3u","text":"Ensure all reagents and materials are prepared and available before starting the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3q44a","text":"Maintain sterile conditions throughout the procedure to prevent contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4agq","text":"Perform all centrifugation steps at the specified speed and duration to ensure consistency.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71muf","text":"Use appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) and adhere to laboratory safety protocols.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fdheh","text":"Include negative controls lacking either the antibody or MNPs to assess non-specific binding.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ehph","text":"Labeled bacterial suspensions can be stored at 4°C for up to 24 hours without significant loss of functionality.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"4o906","text":"Thaw frozen E. coli ATCC 25922 stock on ice.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":12,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ssji","text":"Prepare PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":29,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cv4i0","text":"Prepare 0.5% skimmed milk blocking solution in PBS.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1n5el","text":"Equilibrate all reagents to room temperature unless otherwise specified.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"203C4E252F2611F0B27A0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9bnpp","text":"Handle all bacterial cultures and reagents following biosafety level 2 (BSL-2) guidelines.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5aamb","text":"Dispose of biological waste according to institutional and governmental regulations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4fp","text":"Use appropriate PPE, including lab coats, gloves, and eye protection.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dlupj","text":"Triton‱ X-100 is an irritant; handle with care and avoid inhalation or contact with skin and eyes.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":6,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"237E5E192F2611F0B27A0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}

tips","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A3DAFB342F2611F088F40A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"1":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A3DAFB6F2F2611F088F40A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"2":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A3DAFBA42F2611F088F40A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
steps: Streak E. coli ATCC 25922 from frozen stock onto a PCA plate.
Incubate at 37°C for 24 hours.
Select a single colony and suspend in 1 mL PBS.
Using a cuvette-based spectrophotometer, adjust the optical density at 600 nm to 0.5 to achieve a bacterial concentration of ~1 × 10 8 CFU/mL.
Centrifuge at 3,000 × g for 6 minutes.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Finally, resuspend the pellet in 200 µL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Repeat the wash step twice.
Add 2 µL of rabbit anti- E. coli IgG antibody (4 mg/mL) to the bacterial suspension.
Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes on a rotary agitator.
Centrifuge at 3,000 × g for 3 minutes.
Discard the supernatant and wash the pellet twice with 1 mL PBS containing 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Resuspend the final pellet in 100 µL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Pipette the necesary volume of 100 nm diameter Protein A-coated MNPs into 500 µL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100 to achieve a ratio of 300 MNP per bacterial cell.
Place the mixture in a magnetic concentrator for 2 minutes.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the particles in 500 µL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Repeat the wash step twice.
After the final wash, remove the supernatant and take the tubes off the magnetic separator.
Add the 100 µL antibody-labeled E. coli suspension to the prepared MNPs.
Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes on a rotary agitator.
Centrifuge the mixture at 3,000 × g for 3 minutes.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 0.5% skimmed milk blocking solution.
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes on a rotary agitator.
Centrifuge at 3,000 × g for 3 minutes.
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Place the tube in a magnetic concentrator for 10 minutes.
Carefully pipette the supernatant (containing non-labeled bacteria) into a separate microcentrifuge tube.
Resuspend the pellet (containing labeled bacteria) in 1 mL PBS with 0.01% Triton‱ X-100.
Prepare negative controls by omitting either the antibody or the MNPs during the labeling process to assess non-specific binding.
Perform plate counts on both the supernatant and pellet fractions to determine the number of non-labeled (N F ) and labeled (N L ) bacterial cells, respectively.
Calculate the capture efficiency (CE) using the formula: CE (%) = N L / (N L + N F ) x 100
Store the labeled bacterial suspensions at 4°C.
Use within 24 hours to ensure optimal functionality.
creator: Pedro Fonseca
